title: Time-Invariant and Time-Varying Systems
summary: This page defines time-invariant (constant-parameter) systems as those whose parameters do not change with time, such that a delay in the input produces an equivalent delay in the output. The commutativity of a system S with a time delay is presented as the key property of time-invariant systems, illustrated with block diagrams. A counterexample using the time-varying system y(t) = e^{-t}x(t) demonstrates that this commutativity does not hold for time-varying systems.

figures.caption: Illustration of the time-invariance property showing that for a time-invariant system, delaying the output y(t) by T seconds is equivalent to first delaying the input x(t) by T seconds and then passing it through the system S.
